pages.full_text:
The Viaduct of Garabit (Chantal). Built by Eiffel 1882-4. 565 m. long. across R. Truyere. Designed by Boyer.

We sat with 2 other couples & had:
melon
mushroom omlette (ceps)
stuffed cabbage
cheese
Far Breton? on pastry with raspberries.
photo_captions: The Viaduct of Garabit (Chantal). Built by Eiffel 1882-4. 565 m. long. across R. Truyere. Designed by Boyer.
pages.notes: Page contains a glued-in postcard photograph of 'VIADUC DE GARABIT' (printed text on photo). Handwritten caption is written rotated alongside the postcard. The diarist name 'Chantal' in the caption may refer to a postcard series or series name rather than a person. 'Far Breton' is a traditional French dessert (could also read 'Far Breton'). Partial text visible at gutter edge on right side of image belongs to right page and has not been double-transcribed.

pages.full_text:
During the night there was terrific lightning + the cockerel kept crowing. Rain poured + Charles came back into the van, thank goodness -a strong sense of self-preservation. He then dashed back to get his sleeping bag.
	Having spoken to our neighbours at supper, we decided to try one of the walks.
pages.route_sketch_description: Hand-drawn pencil sketch occupying the upper half of the page, appearing to show a campsite layout or pitch arrangement. Includes a note 'hoops adapter? to camp electric cables' written alongside the sketch. The sketch shows what appear to be lines representing a vehicle/van, a fence or barrier with cross-hatching, and other campsite features. A small label at the bottom of the sketch is illegible.
pages.notes: Upper portion of page contains a hand-drawn pencil sketch of what appears to be a campsite layout; the annotation reads 'hoops adapter? to camp electric cables' — 'hoops' may be '[?hoops]' or '[?loops]'. The sketch label at bottom is illegible. The narrative text below the sketch is clearly written in blue ink. No handwritten page number visible.
